heading: Token Filter Types
body:
MongoDB Search supports the following types of token filter:

- [asciiFolding](asciiFolding-tf-ref)

- [daitchMokotoffSoundex](daitchmokotoffsoundex-tf-ref)

- [edgeGram](edgegram-tf-ref)

- [englishPossessive](englishPossessive-tf-ref)

- [flattenGraph](flattenGraph-tf-ref)

- [icuFolding](icufolding-tf-ref)

- [icuNormalizer](icunormalizer-tf-ref)

- [keywordRepeat](keywordRepeat-tf-ref)

- [kStemming](kStemming-tf-ref)

- [length](length-tf-ref)

- [lowercase](lowercase-tf-ref)

- [regex](regex-tf-ref)

- [removeDuplicates](removeDuplicates-tf-ref)

- [reverse](reverse-tf-ref)

- [shingle](shingle-tf-ref)

- [snowballStemming](snowballstemming-tf-ref)

- [spanishPluralStemming](spanishPluralStemming-tf-ref)

- [stempel](stempel-tf-ref)

- [stopword](stopword-tf-ref)

- [trim](trim-tf-ref)

- [wordDelimiterGraph](wordDelimiterGraph-tf-ref)

The following sample index definitions and queries use the [sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) named `minutes`.
To follow along with these examples, load the `minutes` collection on your cluster
and navigate to the Create a Search Index page in the Atlas UI following the steps
in the [Create a MongoDB Search index](create-fts-index) tutorial.
Then, select the `minutes` collection as your data source, and follow the example procedure
to create an index from the Atlas UI or using [mongosh](mongodb-shell).

drivers

➤ Use the **Select your language** drop-down menu to set the
language of the example on this page.
